mnemonics: PACKAGE PRIVILEGE: Public, Protected, Default, Private - Protect Data Carefully, Please.
keyPoints: A class can be public or have package-private access (no modifier).
Members (fields/methods) have four access levels: public, protected, default (package-private), and private.
A protected member is accessible within its package and by subclasses, even in different packages.
commonMistakes: Assuming a public class makes its members accessible everywhere; members still have their own access levels.
Forgetting that default (package-private) access allows access only within the same package, not subclasses in other packages.
Confusing protected access (package + subclasses) with public or default access.
